Hamamatsu City website [[Category:Shizuoka Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Hamanaku Ward History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Shizuoka Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kitaoka-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kitaoka-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kitaoka-Daizuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kitaoka-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kitaoka-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kitaoka-Daizuka Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:北岡大塚古墳]]

{{Japanese kofun |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |artifacts=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (double-lipped jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of burial mounds on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These burial mounds may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a burial mound{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural
