The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial_facility=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alias= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |construction_period=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound Dimensions == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] Dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} == External Links revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the shape of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === Hamamatsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-typed) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-typed) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Cultural Properties == === But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_designation == === Hamamatsu City Designated other_designation === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_designation == === Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_designation == === Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated other_designation: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City other_designation. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. Artifacts include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City other_designation in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City other_designation. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_designation == === Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated other_designation: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |icon=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designated a Hamamatsu City other_designation. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designated as a Hamamatsu City other_designation in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designated as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City other_designation. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_designation == === But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated other_designation: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |label_position=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been designation1 a Hamamatsu City other_designation. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin formed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was designation1 as a Hamamatsu City other_designation in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also designation1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City other_designation. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_designation == === Hamamatsu City Designated other_designation === * '''other_designation''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated other_other_designation: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been other_designation1 a Hamamatsu City other_other_designation. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was other_designation1 as a Hamamatsu City other_other_designation in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also other_designation1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City other_other_designation. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_other_designation == So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |buried_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated other_other_designation: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_cultural_property= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been other_designation1 a Hamamatsu City other_other_designation. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was other_designation1 as a Hamamatsu City other_other_designation in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also other_designation1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City other_other_designation. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == other_other_designation == === Hamamatsu City Designated other_other_designation === * '''other_other_designation''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage
