But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|12=draft}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|12=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|12=draft}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|12=draft}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 北岡大塚古墳 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=浜松市}} |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 =[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[浜名区]]引佐町[[井伊谷]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 50|緯度秒 = 21.30 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 40|経度秒 = 44.87 |ISO = JP-22 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長49.5m
高さ3.2m（後方部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = ガラス小玉・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 浜松市指定史跡「北岡大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル = 北岡大塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''北岡大塚古墳'''（きたおかおおつかこふん）は、[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[浜名区]]引佐町[[井伊谷]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。浜松市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 静岡県西部、神宮寺川と井伊谷川により形成される小盆地（[[井伊谷]]）の東側丘陵上、盆地を西に見下ろす丘陵先端部を切断して築造された古墳である。これまでに[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）に測量調査が、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）に墳丘確認調査が実施されている{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘周囲には周溝が、その外側には幅2-3メートルの周堤が認められる{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。出土品としては[[土師器]]（二重口縁壺）・ガラス小玉がある{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 この北岡大塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。天竜川以西の西遠江地域では最初に築造された古墳である点、前方後方墳として築造された点で注目される古墳になる。また丘陵上では北岡大塚古墳のほかにも古墳の分布が知られ、北岡大塚古墳→馬場平古墳→馬場平3号墳→谷津古墳の順の首長墓系譜が想定される。これらの古墳については、井伊谷北西部にある古墳時代の祭祀遺跡である[[天白磐座遺跡]]との関連性も指摘される辰巳和弘『聖なる水の祀りと古代王権 天白磐座遺跡（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」33）』新泉社、2006年。。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に浜松市指定史跡に指定されている。なお、付近には[[終末期古墳]]である北岡2号墳（浜松市指定史跡）がある。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年） ** 古墳として周知{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（平凡社）|2000}}。 ** 5月23日、浜松市指定史跡に指定。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、測量調査（引佐町教育委員会、[[1981年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）、墳丘確認調査（引佐町教育委員会、[[1996年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 墳丘 == [[ファイル:北岡大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|浜松市地域遺産センター展示。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：49.5メートル * 後方部 ** 長さ：29.5メートル ** 幅：29メートル ** 高さ：3.2メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：20.0メートル ** 幅：23.5メートル ** 高さ：2.8メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：20メートル == 文化財 == === 浜松市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 北岡大塚古墳 - 1979年（昭和54年）5月23日指定[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html 北岡大塚古墳]（浜松市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 浜松市地域遺産センター（浜松市浜名区引佐町井伊谷） - 北岡大塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（浜松市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=北岡大塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|北岡大塚古墳（平凡社）|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=川江秀孝|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 北岡大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|北岡大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=川江秀孝|year=2002|chapter=大塚古墳 > 北岡大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|北岡大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=辰巳和弘|authorlink=辰巳和弘|year=1981|chapter=北岡大塚古墳（前方後方墳）測量調査報告書|title=引佐町の古墳文化II（引佐町町史編纂室報告 第2冊)）|publisher=引佐町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author1=辰巳和弘|authorlink1=辰巳和弘|author2=日高慎|year=1996|chapter=|title=北岡大塚古墳 -第2次調査報告書-（引佐町の遺跡7）|publisher=引佐町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[天白磐座遺跡]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html 北岡大塚古墳] - 浜松市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:きたおかおおつかこふん}} [[Category:静岡県の古墳]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:静岡県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|12=draft}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|12=draft}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北岡大塚古墳|version=99995642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shizuoka Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Hamanaku Ward History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Shizuoka Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kitaoka-Daizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:北岡大塚古墳]] [[sn:itaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]]

The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanaku Ward|qq=}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanaku Ward|qq=}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanaku Ward|qq=}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanaku Ward|qq=}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location={{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanaku Ward|qq=}}, Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of {{ill|Hamanaku Ward|ja|浜名区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanaku Ward|qq=}}. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) nor {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) -
