revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:浜名区の歴史
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location=[[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]], Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北岡大塚古墳|version=99995642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Hamanaku Ward History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:北岡大塚古墳]] [[sn:itaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location=[[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]], Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北岡大塚古墳|version=99995642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Hamanaku Ward History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:北岡大塚古墳]] [[sn:itaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:静岡県の古墳]]

It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location=[[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]], Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 引佐町井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun template and pages that transclude it
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location=[[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]], Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 引佐町井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北岡大塚古墳|version=99995642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:北岡大塚古墳]] [[sn:itaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Kofun of Shizuoka prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県内の市町村指定史跡]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location=[[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]], Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 引佐町井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北岡大塚古墳|version=99995642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:北岡大塚古墳]] [[sn:itaoka Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Kofun of Shizuoka prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県内の市町村指定史跡]]

The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Second Excavation Report - (Archaeological Sites of Inasa Town 7)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |image={{image Needed|cat=Hamamatsu}} |alternate_name= |location=[[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]], Inasa-chō {{ill|Ii-no-Tani|ja|井伊谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ii-no-Tani|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 引佐町井伊谷}} |coordinates={{coord|34|50|21.30|N|137|40|44.87|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) |size=Total mound length: 49.5 m
Rear section height: 3.2 m |burial=Unknown |burial goods=Glass beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite_kofun= |built=Mid-4th century |Interred_individual= |imperial_grave= |historic_site=Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site: Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun |tangible_notes= |notes= |map=Japan Shizuoka |type=Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label=Kitaoka Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun''' (北岡大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani district of [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaku Ward]]. The mound is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (rectangular keyhole-shaped) and has been Historic Site1 a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. == Overview == Located on the eastern ridge of a small basin typeed by the Jinguji River and Ii-no-Tani River, the Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun was constructed by cutting into the terminal edge of the ridge overlooking the basin to the west. In 1980, a survey was conducted, followed by a mound confirmation investigation in 1994{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound takes the type of a rectangular keyhole, with the rectangular front section oriented westward{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) were found on the mound's surface{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}. Surrounding the mound is a moat, with an outer embankment 2–3 meters wide{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial facility has not been excavated. burial goods include [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (double-lidded jars) and small glass beads{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the mid-4th century{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It is notable as the earliest kofun built west of the Tenryū River in the Nishitōtōmi region and as an example of the rectangular keyhole mound type. The distribution of Kofuns on the ridge suggests a lineage of chieftains, progressing from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun to Babahira Kofun, Babahira No. 3 Kofun, and finally Yatsu Kofun. These Kofuns may be associated with the ritual site {{ill|Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwakura Site|lt=Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|ja|天白磐座遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins|qq=}} in the northwestern part of Ii-no-TaniTatsumi Kazuhiro, "Sacred Water Rites and Ancient Authority: Tenpaku Iwaza Ruins," Shinsensha, 2006.. The site was Historic Site1 as a Hamamatsu City Historic Site in 1979. Nearby is Kitaoka No. 2 Kofun, a terminal-period kofun also Historic Site1 as a historic site. == History == * '''1979''': ''' Recognized as a Kofun{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}. ''' May 23: Designated a Hamamatsu City Historic Site. * '''1980''': Survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1981{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''1994''': Mound confirmation survey conducted by the Inasa Town Board of Education; report published in 1996{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. == Mound area == [[File:北岡大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|excavations}}{{small|Displayed at Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center.}}]] area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Total length''': 49.5 meters * '''Rear section''': ''' Length: 29.5 meters ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 3.2 meters * '''Front section''': ''' Length: 20.0 meters ''' Width: 23.5 meters ''' Height: 2.8 meters * '''Neck section''': ''' Width: 20 meters == Historic Site == === Hamamatsu City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''': ''' Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Designated May 23, 1979[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/shitei/inasa/inasa/kitaoka.html Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun] - Hamamatsu City website.. == Related Facilities == * '''Hamamatsu City Regional Heritage Center''' (Hamamatsu, Hamanaku Ward, Inasa-chō Ii-no-Tani) - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun - Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Hamamatsu City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 22: Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|2000}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|editor=|author=Kawae Hidetaka|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|Additional Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuddan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|year=1981|chapter=Survey Report of Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun (Zenpō-kōhō-fun)|title=Kofun Culture in Inasa Town II (Inasa Town Historical Report Series No. 2)|publisher=Inasa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|authorlink=Tatsumi Kazuhiro|coauthor=Hidaka Shin|year=1996|chapter=|title=Kitaoka Ōtsuka Kofun -
